thesis.core_idea: India's largest private sector bank with strong fundamentals but facing near-term headwinds from high loan-to-deposit ratio and profit growth moderation
thesis.growth_driver: Retail banking expansion, digital transformation, post-merger HDFC Ltd integration synergies, growing insurance distribution
thesis.edge: Brand strength, technology leadership, largest retail deposit base, extensive distribution network, superior digital platform adoption
thesis.trigger: CD ratio stabilization to 85-90% by FY2027, deposit growth acceleration, margin improvement, resolution of governance concerns

quality.governance_flags: Chairman resignation in March 2026; CEO extension pending RBI approval; governance transparency concerns

capital_efficiency.reinvestment_quality: Moderate; high CD ratio constraining growth investments

structural_risks: Deposit growth lag vs loan demand
Net Interest Margin compression (3.6% in FY24)
cyclical_risks: Credit cycle downturn risk amid macro slowdown
Asset quality deterioration in MSME/auto segments
US tariff impact on corporate credit quality
company_risks: Governance disclosure gaps creating investor concern
Execution risk on CD ratio normalization

position_sizing.suggested_allocation: For Saksham: Increase allocation from 2.5% to 5-6% on dips to 750-800; target portfolio weight of 9.1% should be achieved incrementally

next_triggers: Q4 FY26 results (Apr 2026): Focus on CD ratio trajectory, deposit growth rate, margin trends
New Chairman appointment and governance clarity
RBI guidance on deposit growth requirements
Credit growth slowdown data for system
key_metrics: Loan-to-Deposit ratio trend (target: 90-96% for FY26, 85-90% for FY27)

data_sources: HDFC Bank Q3 FY26 earnings presentation
HDFC Bank management earnings call transcript (Jan 2026)
NSE official announcements (Chairman resignation, CEO extension)
